content:
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Matt Hasan
Every executive says they have an AI strategy.
Far fewer have a strategy for improving human judgment.
That is the real competitive divide.
As AI becomes capable of producing competent analyses, reports, code, and recommendations in seconds, the value of simply generating answers declines. The scarce resource becomes asking better questions, challenging assumptions, integrating context, and making wiser decisions.
Organizations that treat AI as a cheaper employee will automate yesterday’s work.
Organizations that treat AI as a cognitive partner will redefine tomorrow’s work.
The future belongs to leaders who know the difference.
